essence: Builds AI‑enhanced robotic systems that adapt, learn, and optimise their behaviour in advanced manufacturing environments.

identity.title: AI Robotics Engineer
identity.summary: Designs, programs, and deploys robotics systems powered by machine learning, enabling autonomous manipulation, adaptive control, and intelligent interaction with CNC and production equipment.

salary.notes: Engineers in aerospace, robotics, or fully autonomous smart‑factory environments can exceed £165k-£230k+.

qualifications: Experience in robotics engineering, automation, or AI‑driven control systems
Knowledge of robot kinematics, motion planning, and sensor integration
Skills in machine learning, reinforcement learning, and adaptive control
Understanding of CNC, manufacturing workflows, and Industry 4.0 systems

lifestyle.description: AI robotics engineers work in aerospace, automotive, robotics, and Industry 4.0 environments. They build intelligent robotic systems that adapt to real‑time conditions, collaborate with CNC machines, and support autonomous manufacturing. The role suits individuals who enjoy robotics, AI, and high‑tech engineering challenges.

habits: Designs and programs AI‑enhanced robotic systems
Implements reinforcement learning for adaptive behaviour
Integrates robotics with CNC, automation, and digital manufacturing
Develops perception systems using sensors and machine vision
Ensures safe, efficient, autonomous robotic operation
